description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a robust, metallic head. The head consists of a flat, circular striking face on one side and a curved, bifurcated claw on the opposite side for nail removal. The handle is straight and tapers slightly towards the end, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The metal head is dark in color, contrasting with the lighter wood of the handle, and appears securely attached. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for construction, carpentry, or general repair tasks in urban environments. No visible branding, wear, or additional attachments are present.
description: A traditional claw hammer with a light wooden handle and a dark metal head, featuring a flat striking face and a curved claw for nail removal.
description_view_0: The hammer is shown from the front, highlighting the flat striking face and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: This side view displays the bifurcated claw and the cylindrical shape of the hammer head, as well as the handle's grain.
description_view_2: The hammer is seen from the side, emphasizing the length of the handle and the profile of the head and claw.
description_view_3: A top-down view reveals the split claw and the rounded striking face, with the handle extending away.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with visible grain, slightly tapered and smooth, no additional grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: claw hammer head with a flat striking face and a curved, split claw for nail removal, made of steel
